details.template_tested: duckduckgo_sse native format: data: {role/message/id JSON} + data: [DONE]

eventstream.note: Native DuckDuckGo server format successfully parsed as 2 SSE events

response_headers.x-vqd-hash-1: MISSING — not present in block response

ui_display.error_text: 이런... Duck.ai를 일시적으로 사용할 수 없습니다. 잠시 후 다시 시도하세요.

analysis.hypothesis: Native DuckDuckGo format (role/message/id JSON + [DONE]) would pass app action validation
analysis.result: DISPROVED — ERR_INVALID_ACTION persists with native format
analysis.root_cause_narrowing: ERR_INVALID_ACTION is NOT about SSE format (Vercel AI SDK vs native). The app validates 'actions' at a higher abstraction level — likely the Vercel AI SDK client library wrapping the fetch, which expects specific protocol framing (0:"text", d:{finish}, etc.) regardless of the raw SSE data content.
key_findings: Native format parsed correctly as 2 SSE events (same as Vercel format in #425)
ERR_INVALID_ACTION triggered identically for both Vercel AI SDK format AND native server format
x-vqd-hash-1 header still MISSING from block response
The DuckDuckGo client uses Vercel AI SDK as middleware — it expects Vercel protocol framing, NOT raw server format
SSE format is irrelevant — the action validation happens in the Vercel AI SDK layer BEFORE app logic processes the message content
analysis.conclusion: DuckDuckGo wraps its chat API through Vercel AI SDK on the client side. The SDK validates the response against its own protocol spec (0: text-delta, d: finish, etc.) and throws ERR_INVALID_ACTION when the response doesn't match any known 'action' type. Neither native server format NOR Vercel format with custom content passes this validation. The block response must perfectly mimic Vercel AI SDK protocol WITH valid action types.
next_steps: Capture a REAL successful DuckDuckGo response to see exact Vercel AI SDK action envelope structure
Check if x-vqd-hash-1 absence contributes to the error (may need HAR from unblocked response)
Investigate Vercel AI SDK source code for action type validation logic
Consider whether DuckDuckGo warning requires JS injection approach instead of SSE response replacement

summary: FAILURE — Native DuckDuckGo format does NOT resolve ERR_INVALID_ACTION. The error is in the Vercel AI SDK action validation layer, not in SSE format parsing. Both native and Vercel formats fail identically. The block response template must match Vercel AI SDK protocol action types exactly, or an alternative approach (JS injection) may be needed.
